summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #588 where `is_container_available()` ignores Podman as a container backend, causing Podman users to be blocked before `detect_backend()` ever runs.
summary.outcome: Added Podman to the availability check, updated two error/warning strings to mention Podman, verified clean compilation, committed, and prepared to push and open a PR.

repo: Container backend detection is split between two functions: `is_container_available()` (gate check) and `detect_backend()` (actual selection). These must be kept in sync when adding new backends.
The moltis repo uses a worktree-based workflow under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/.
Pre-existing cargo warnings are present in the codebase and are not introduced by this change.

code.finding: `is_container_available()` only checked Docker and Apple Container (macOS), missing Podman. Fix adds `ContainerBackend::Podman.is_available()` check.
Early availability guard that short-circuits before `detect_backend()` runs. Error message incorrectly listed only Docker and Apple Container.
Warning message also omitted Podman from the list of supported container backends.

workflow: cargo check was slow on first run but fast on subsequent runs due to caching — no need to wait between retries.
The fix required no structural changes; the downstream container launch and image handling code already supported Podman correctly.

friction: cargo check took multiple invocations before completing, requiring the assistant to re-run it and wait.
open_items: PR was not yet created/pushed at transcript end — push and PR creation steps were initiated but not confirmed complete.
Consider adding a test or assertion that `is_container_available()` and `detect_backend()` stay in sync when new backends are added.
